events.title: BT Nautilus spills heavy fuel oil after grounding in Kill Van Kull
events.description: Reports said the tanker BT Nautilus ran aground in the Kill Van Kull waterway and spilled about 260,000 gallons of No. 6 (heavy) fuel oil. The spill contaminated the channel and prompted Coast Guard response and cleanup activities, with an investigation reported the next day.

location.name: Kill Van Kull waterway (between Bayonne, New Jersey and Staten Island, New York), USA

sources.headline: 260,000 gallons of fuel oil spill into N.Y.-area channel
Investigation announced for busy Kill Van Kull

key_quotes.quote: 260,000 gallons of heavy fuel oil spilled into the Kill Van Kull channel
Investigation announced for busy Kill Van Kull

dedupe.cluster_hint: Kill Van Kull BT Nautilus oil spill (June 1990)

events.title: Lower Ohio Valley tornado outbreak (June 2–3, 1990)
events.description: A widespread severe-weather outbreak produced numerous tornadoes across parts of the Midwest and Ohio Valley on June 2–3, 1990, with extensive wind damage reported. The cited NWS page documents the event impacts in the affected region.

sources.headline: June 2, 1990 Tornadoes

key_quotes.quote: severe-weather outbreak produced numerous tornadoes across the Midwest and Ohio Valley

events.title: Flash flooding from severe thunderstorms in eastern Ohio (June 14–15, 1990)
events.description: Severe thunderstorms triggered deadly flash flooding in parts of eastern Ohio around June 14–15, 1990. The referenced USGS report documents extreme peak discharges in the affected basins, with recurrence intervals exceeding 100 years in places.

location.name: Ohio, USA (eastern counties; Pipe & Wegee Creek basins near Shadyside)

sources.headline: Water-Resources Investigations Report 91-4147

key_quotes.quote: deadly flooding and extreme peak discharges with recurrence intervals exceeding 100 years

events.title: Flood in the Clear Creek Basin near Coralville, Iowa (June 17, 1990)
events.description: A significant flood occurred in the Clear Creek Basin near Coralville, Iowa on June 17, 1990. The cited USGS publication documents the event and reports a flood-peak discharge at the Clear Creek gage with an estimated recurrence interval of about 80 years.

location.name: Iowa, USA (Clear Creek Basin near Coralville)

sources.headline: Flood of June 17, 1990, in the Clear Creek Basin, East-Central Iowa

key_quotes.quote: Flood of June 17, 1990, in the Clear Creek Basin, East-Central Iowa

events.title: Southwest U.S. extreme heat episode (late June 1990)
events.description: An intense upper-level high produced extreme heat across the U.S. Southwest in late June 1990, with records reported in multiple locations. The referenced NOAA/WPC training summary notes record temperatures including Phoenix reaching 122°F on June 26 and Downtown Los Angeles reaching 112°F.

sources.headline: June 1990 Heat Wave (training material)

key_quotes.quote: Phoenix reaching 122°F on June 26 and Downtown Los Angeles reaching 112°F

events.title: Panay Island earthquake (Mw 7.1) — Western Visayas, Philippines
events.description: A shallow tectonic earthquake struck Panay Island in the Philippines on June 14, 1990, causing deaths, injuries, and damage. Reports described structural impacts and ground disturbances including landslides in affected areas.

sources.headline: 1990 June 14 Panay Earthquake
Earthquakes (USGS publications) — 1990 issues

events.title: Rudbar–Manjil (Northern Iran) earthquake (M~7.4–7.7) and associated Caspian Sea tsunami
events.description: A major earthquake struck northern Iran on June 20, 1990, causing widespread destruction and ground failures such as landslides. Studies have described a localized tsunami in the Caspian Sea affecting sections of the southern shoreline.

location.name: Rudbar–Manjil area, Gilan/Zanjan, Iran (Caspian Sea region)

sources.headline: This Day in Earthquake History (June 20)
Article in Geophysical Journal International (vol. 201, issue 2, p. 621)

events.title: Dude Fire blowup in Tonto National Forest kills six firefighters
events.description: The Dude Fire in the Payson Ranger District of Arizona’s Tonto National Forest intensified rapidly and overran firefighting crews, resulting in six firefighter fatalities and multiple injuries. Reporting places the fatal blowup on June 26, 1990 following ignition the prior day.

location.name: Payson Ranger District, Tonto National Forest (near Payson), Arizona, USA

sources.headline: Dude Fire Arizona — June 26, 1990
Six firefighters reported killed in wildfire

key_quotes.quote: Six firefighters reported killed in wildfire

events.title: Painted Cave Fire spreads into Santa Barbara area under strong winds
events.description: A fast-moving wildfire started near Painted Cave Road/Highway 154 in the Santa Ynez Mountains and spread rapidly under hot, dry, windy conditions into neighborhoods in the Santa Barbara area. Reports described evacuations and extensive structure losses as the fire advanced.

location.name: Santa Ynez Mountains / Santa Barbara County, California, USA

sources.headline: (LA Times archive link)
35 years since the Painted Cave Fire

key_quotes.quote: 35 years since the Painted Cave Fire

events.title: Brush fire in Glendale damages or destroys homes; arson suspected
events.description: A brush fire in Glendale, California spread rapidly and damaged or destroyed multiple homes. Reporting indicated investigators found signs consistent with deliberate ignition and authorities searched for an arson suspect.

sources.headline: Wildfires rage through the West
(LA Times archive link)

events.title: Carbon Canyon wildfire forces evacuations and destroys homes near Brea/Chino Hills
events.description: A wildfire in the Carbon Canyon area near Brea and Chino Hills blackened thousands of acres, forced evacuations, and destroyed multiple homes. Reports also described an arson arrest in connection with the fire’s ignition.

location.name: Carbon Canyon area near Brea and Chino Hills, California, USA

sources.headline: (LA Times archive link)
(LA Times archive link)

events.title: Carbon Canyon fire reported contained after burning 6,640 acres and damaging homes
events.description: A major brush fire in the Carbon Canyon area near Brea was reported contained on June 29, 1990 after burning thousands of acres and destroying or damaging homes. Follow-up reporting summarized the burned area as 6,640 acres and cited 14 homes destroyed or damaged.

sources.headline: (LA Times archive link)
(LA Times archive link)

events.title: Northern spotted owl listed as threatened under the U.S. Endangered Species Act
events.description: The U.S. Fish and Wildlife Service announced the northern spotted owl was listed as a threatened species under the Endangered Species Act. The listing reflected concern over loss of old-growth forest habitat and triggered federal protections.

sources.headline: Northern Spotted Owl Listed As Threatened Species June 22, 1990
Northern Spotted Owl Listed As Threatened Species June 22, 1990

key_quotes.quote: Northern Spotted Owl Listed As Threatened Species June 22, 1990

dedupe.fingerprint: northern spotted owl listed as threatened under the u.s. endangered species act|united states|1990-06-22

events.title: Suspected pesticide/insecticide spill causes fish kill on the Weber River near Ogden, Utah
events.description: Officials reported a toxic spill on Utah's lower Weber River that killed scores of fish including trout and other species. Wildlife officials suspected pesticides or insecticides and advised people not to eat fish found along the affected stretch.

location.name: Lower Weber River near Ogden/Riverdale, Utah, USA

sources.headline: Scores of fish killed by toxic spill; wildlife officials suspect pesticides

key_quotes.quote: Scores of fish killed by toxic spill; wildlife officials suspect pesticides

dedupe.fingerprint: suspected pesticide/insecticide spill causes fish kill on the weber river near ogden, utah|lower weber river near ogden/riverdale, utah, usa|1990-06-05

events.title: EPA final rule on Land Disposal Restrictions for 'Third Third' scheduled hazardous wastes (RCRA)
events.description: EPA published a final rule establishing land disposal restriction treatment standards and effective dates for the 'Third Third' of scheduled hazardous wastes under RCRA. The draft cites EPA pages that index or describe the rulemaking and related authorization materials.

sources.headline: List of Federal Register Notices Pertaining to Hazardous Waste
Hazardous Waste Rules (archived index)
RCRA rule checklists and applications for state authorization

events.title: Montreal Protocol Parties adopt the 1990 London Amendment (ozone-depleting substances controls)
events.description: Parties to the Montreal Protocol adopted the London Amendment, strengthening international controls on ozone-depleting substances and related measures. The draft points to UN treaty records and related treaty documentation describing the amendment.

sources.headline: Montreal Protocol: London Amendment (1990) - treaty details
Treaty Document (102nd Congress) related to the Montreal Protocol London Amendment
London Amendment to the Montreal Protocol (1990) - key document page

events.title: EPA final rule sets organic air emission standards at hazardous waste TSDFs (RCRA Subpart CC)
events.description: EPA published final standards to limit organic air emissions from process vents and equipment leaks at hazardous waste treatment, storage, and disposal facilities regulated under RCRA. The draft notes this as published at 55 FR 25454 and cites EPA pages describing the standards and related rule listings.

sources.headline: List of Federal Register Notices Pertaining to Hazardous Waste
RCRA rule checklists and applications for state authorization
Hazardous waste TSDFs: Organic air emission standards (Subpart CC)
